title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 4 4 20260522

questions.question: In a gaming zone, there are five different games and each game rewards a different number of points which is among 5, 7, 10, 15 and 25 when a player plays that particular game. Rahul played each game thrice. What is the total number of points he earned?
questions.explanation: Here, the total number of points earned by Rahul = (5 + 7 + 10 + 15 + 25) x 3 = 62 x 3 = 186

questions.question: Select the number from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. 3, 4.5, 6.75, 10.12, ?
Find the odd word from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: All other scientists are related to Physics whereas Charles Darwin is related to Biology.

questions.question: Marco's spouse Neha has only one brother, Arjun. Arjun's daughter is Sara. How is Sara related to Marco?
questions.explanation: Marco's spouse Neha has only one brother, Arjun. Arjun's daughter is Sara. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28449/image033.png) Sara is Marco's spouse's sibling's daughter.

questions.question: Find the odd one out of the following numbers.
questions.explanation: Except 391, all other numbers are prime numbers.

questions.question: Which of these words has all its letters arranged alphabetically?
questions.explanation: In the word Glory, the letters appear in alphabetical order as G < L < O < R < Y. In Earth, the sequence breaks because E > A. In Clear, the sequence breaks because L > E. In Ridge, the sequence breaks because R > I. Hence, Glory is the only word whose letters are arranged alphabetically.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third word in the same way as the second word is related to the first word. ( The words must be considered as meaningful English words and must not be related to each other based on the number of letters/numbers of consonants/vowels in the word) Reservoir : Water :: Warehouse : ?
questions.explanation: A reservoir stores water. A warehouse stores goods. Therefore, Warehouse:Goods mirrors the container - content relation.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs matches a device with its primary operation?
questions.explanation: A printer copies or prints documents. A telescope is used for observing, not calculating. A compass aids navigation but does not sail. A stove heats, not cools. Hence, option C is correct.

questions.question: Select the letter-cluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series: K M O, M O Q, ?, Q S U, S U W
questions.explanation: ABCDEFGHIJKLM 12345678910111213 NOPQRSTUVWXYZ 14151617181920212223242526 K + 2 = M; M + 2 = O; O + 2 = Q; Q + 2 = S M + 2 = O; O + 2 = Q; Q + 2 = S; S + 2 = U O + 2 = Q; Q + 2 = S; S + 2 = U; U + 2 = W Hence, the missing letter-cluster is OQS.

questions.question: If A ∧ B means "A is the brother of B," A * B means "A is the wife of B," A @ B means "A is the father of B," and A $ B means "A is the daughter of B," which expression implies H is the brother-in-law of K?
questions.explanation: A. H ∧ M @ K H ∧ M means H is the brother of M M @ K means M is the father of K ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30053/image002.png) B. H ∧ M * K H ∧ M means H is the brother of M M * K means M is the wife of K ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30053/image003.png) Therefore H is the brother of K's wife, i.e., K's brother-in-law (correct option) C. H $ M @ K H $ M means H is the daughter of M M @ K means M is the father of K ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30053/image004.png) D. K * H @ M K * H means K is the wife of H H @ M means H is the father of M ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30053/image005.png)

questions.question: In the question given below, a group of three numbers/symbols is given in each option, Identify the group that does NOT follow the same pattern as the others.
questions.explanation: Option (a) P17 : T22 : X27 P+4= T; T+4= X Also, P+1= 16+1= 17 and 17+5 = 22; 22+5= 27 Option (b) J11 : N16 : R21 J+4= N; N+4= R Also, J+1= 10+1 = 11 and 11+5= 16; 16+5= 21 Option (c) B4 : F9 : J14 B+4= F; F+4= J Also, B+1= 2+1 = 3. So, option (c) does not follow the same pattern. Option (d) L13 : P18 : T23 L+4= P; P+4= T Also, L+1= 12+1 = 13 and 13+5= 18; 18+5= 23

questions.question: Read the given statement and courses of action carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statement is true, decide which of the given courses of action logically follows for pursuing. Statement: Multiple accidents have occurred at an unmanned railway crossing because many commuters ignore warning signs, especially during peak hours. The crossing is the main access route for two nearby villages. Course of action I: Install automatic barriers with warning alarms and deploy cameras to fine repeated violators. Course of action II: Permanently close the crossing immediately, forcing villagers to take a much longer alternate route.
options: Only course of action II follows
Only course of action I follows
questions.explanation: Course of action I improves safety without blocking essential access, and directly matches the problem of rule-breaking and accidents. Course of action II is an extreme step because the crossing is the main access route; immediate permanent closure creates a major hardship and is not a balanced first response. Therefore, only course of action I follows.

questions.question: Complete the series: 5, 9, 17, 33, 65, __
questions.explanation: Add consecutive powers of 2: 5 + 22 = 9 9 + 23 = 17 17 + 24 = 33 33 + 25 = 65 65 + 26 = 129 So the next term is 129.

questions.question: Find the next letter pair in the series: BF, EK, IQ, NX, __
questions.explanation: Write letter positions: B=2, F=6; E=5, K=11; I=9, Q=17; N=14, X=24. First letters: +3, +4, +5; next +6 => 14+6=20 => T. Second letters: +5, +6, +7; next +8 => 24+8=32 => 32−26=6 => F. So the next pair is TF.

questions.question: Pointing to an old man, Rohit said, "His daughter is my wife's mother." How is the old man related to Rohit's wife?
questions.explanation: Old man's daughter = wife's mother. So the old man is wife's maternal grandfather (grandfather). ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30305/image016.gif) Hence, Option C is correct.

questions.question: Select the term that is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term. PALINDROME : EMORDNILAP :: HANDSHAKE : ?
questions.explanation: Pattern: the second word is the reverse of the first. HANDSHAKE reversed → EKAHSDNAH.

questions.question: On a bookshelf, 39 files are arranged in a single row. File T is 15th from the left end and File U is 18th from the right end. How many files are between T and U?
questions.explanation: Let N = total files = 39 Position of U from the left = N − 18 + 1 = 39 − 18 + 1 = 22 Files between T and U = 22 − 15 − 1 = 6

questions.question: In the given question, there is a certain relationship between two given letter pairs on one side of (::) and a pair that is given on another side of (::) where another pair is to be found from the given alternatives, having the same relationship with this pair as the pairs of the given pair bear. Choose the best alternative. DIOR : FLSX :: BEST : ?
questions.explanation: We know that, AlphabetsABCDEFGHIJKLM Positional value12345678910111213 AlphabetsZYXWVUTSRQPON Positional value26252423222120191817161514 Here, the logic is: D+2= F; I+3= L; O+4= S; R+6= X. Similarly, B+2= D; E+3= H; S+4= W; T+6= Z.

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'Bright future awaits' is coded as 't9@ m4# k2%', 'Future success matters' is coded as 't9@ r5* s6+' and 'Awaits success today' is coded as 'k2% d8~ s6+'. What is the code for 'Today'?
questions.explanation: In a certain code language, 'Bright future awaits' is coded as 't9@ m4# k2%', 'Future success matters' is coded as 't9@ r5* s6+' and 'Awaits success today' is coded as 'k2% d8~ s6+'. The code for 'Future' = t9@ The code for 'Awaits' = k2% The code for 'Success' = s6+ Thus, the code for 'Today' = d8~

questions.question: Tarun, who is the son of only daughter of Harish, is married to Neha's sister. Nita is the only sister of Neha. How is Nita related to Harish's son-in-law?
questions.explanation: Tarun, who is the son of only daughter of Harish, is married to Neha's sister. Nita is the only sister of Neha. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30405/image026.png) Harish has only one daughter that means he has only one son-in-law. So, Nita will be the daughter-in-law of Harish's son-in-law.

questions.question: Select the correct option that gives the correct water image of the given figure: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14662/image025.jpg) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14662/image026.png)
questions.explanation: The correct water image is: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14662/image027.png)

questions.question: If '*' means addition, '&' means subtraction, '@' means multiplication and '$' means division, then what is the value of (15 * 5) @ 2 $ 5 & 3 * 4?

Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All bats are birds. Some rats are bats. Conclusions: I. Some rats are birds. II. Some birds are bats. III. All rats are bats.
options: Only conclusions I and II follow.
Only conclusions I and III follow.
questions.explanation: It is given that, All bats are birds. Some rats are bats. The minimum overlapping diagram is, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19993/image006.jpg) Here, some rats are birds and some birds are bats, and all rats are not bats. Hence, only conclusions I and II follow.

questions.question: Which letter-cluster will replace the question mark (?) in the following series? DF, JL, PR, ?
questions.explanation: D (E) F (GHI) J (K) L (MNO) P (Q) R (STU) V (W) X

questions.question: A situation is given, followed by two conclusions I and II. Read the situation and both the conclusions carefully and select the correct answer. Situation: Despite numerous healthcare reforms in India over the last decade, many rural areas still lack access to basic medical facilities, resulting in a higher mortality rate than urban regions Conclusions: I. Healthcare reforms in India have not been successful in improving rural healthcare. II. Urban regions have benefitted more from healthcare reforms than rural areas.
options: Both I and II can be concluded
Neither I nor II can be concluded
questions.explanation: Conclusion I: The statement indicates a gap in rural healthcare, but it doesn't state that the reforms have been unsuccessful overall. Conclusion II: Since the situation describes better healthcare in urban areas compared to rural ones, this can be concluded. Therefore, only II can be concluded.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the first-ever woman brand ambassador of Punjab National Bank (PNB) to lead its innovation-driven customer outreach?
questions.explanation: Harmanpreet Kaur has been appointed as the first-ever woman brand ambassador of Punjab National Bank (PNB). Her leadership, consistency, and modern sporting image align with PNB's goal of innovation-driven customer engagement and youth outreach. Further Insights PNB is one of India's oldest public sector banks. Brand ambassadors help banks improve trust and visibility. Sports personalities are often chosen for their discipline and leadership image. Women-centric branding reflects inclusive growth messaging.

questions.question: According to the 2011 Census, which of the following States/Union Territories recorded the highest literacy rate in India(excluding Kerala)?
questions.explanation: As per the 2011 Census, Lakshadweep recorded a literacy rate of around 92.3%, the highest in India after Kerala. This places Lakshadweep above highly literate states like Mizoram and Goa. Further Insights Kerala recorded the highest literacy rate overall in 2011. Literacy rate measures population aged 7 years and above. Small populations often show better education indicators. Southern and North-Eastern regions dominate top literacy rankings.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the human glandular system: i) Endocrine glands secrete hormones directly into the bloodstream to act as chemical messengers. ii) Exocrine glands secrete substances like enzymes, sweat, and saliva through ducts onto an epithelial surface. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement (i) is correct: Endocrine glands are ductless glands. They release hormones directly into the blood, which then act on specific target organs. Statement (ii) is correct: Exocrine glands release substances like digestive enzymes, sweat, and saliva through ducts onto body surfaces or into cavities. Further Insights Examples of endocrine glands: pituitary, thyroid, adrenal. Examples of exocrine glands: salivary, sweat, pancreatic (exocrine part). Hormones regulate growth, metabolism, and reproduction. Glandular system questions are common in prelims biology. Endocrine signals usually act slower but last longer.

questions.question: Who among the following was the first person of Indian origin to win the Booker Prize?
questions.explanation: V.S. Naipaul won the Booker Prize in 1971 for In a Free State. He was the first person of Indian origin to receive this award (of Indian diaspora background). Salman Rushdie Salman Rushdie won the Booker Prize in 1981 (Midnight's Children). Arundhati Roy Arundhati Roy won in 1997 (The God of Small Things) Kiran Desai Kiran Desai won in 2006 (The Inheritance of Loss). Further Insights The Booker Prize is awarded for English-language fiction. Salman Rushdie's Midnight's Children also won the Booker of Bookers. Arundhati Roy remains the first Indian citizen to win the Booker. Kiran Desai is the youngest woman to have won the Booker Prize.

questions.question: Which of the following is the primary accompanying instrument for Odissi dance and was also historically used in Dhrupad music?
questions.explanation: Pakhawaj is a barrel-shaped percussion instrument traditionally used in Odissi dance performances. It was the principal percussion instrument in Dhrupad music before the widespread use of the tabla. The rhythmic patterns (talas) of Odissi are deeply rooted in Pakhawajbols. Further Insights Dhrupad is the oldest surviving form of Hindustani classical music. Tabla evolved later as a lighter alternative to Pakhawaj. Odissi is known for tribhangi and chowk postures. Pakhawaj produces deep bass sounds, ideal for temple traditions. Classical dances preserve regional musical heritage.

questions.question: Read the following statements carefully and choose the correct option regarding ASSERTION (A) andREASON (R) Assertion (A): Article 263 existed in the Constitution since 1950, a permanent Inter-State Council was established only in 1980. Reason (R): The Sarkaria Commission recommended the setting up of a permanent Inter-State Council to promote cooperative federalism and resolve disputes between the Union and the States.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: Why Assertion (A) is false Article 263 has indeed been part of the Constitution since 1950, empowering the President to establish an Inter-State Council. However, the permanent Inter-State Council was established in 1990, not in 1980. It was set up by a Presidential Order, following later recommendations. Why Reason (R) is true The Sarkaria Commission (1983 - 1988) examined Centre - State relations. It strongly recommended the creation of a permanent Inter-State Council. The objective was to promote cooperative federalism and provide a forum for policy coordination and dispute resolution. Further Insights The Inter-State Council is a constitutional body, not a statutory one. It is chaired by the Prime Minister of India. The Council discusses policy issues of common interest. It plays a key role in cooperative federalism. Article 263 makes the Council advisory in nature.

questions.question: Which of the following places is associated with the production of India's first aluminium ingot in 1943?
questions.explanation: Alupuram Works, located in Ernakulam district, Kerala, was established in 1938 by the Indian Aluminium Company Limited (INDAL) and produced India's first aluminium ingot on March 6, 1943. Further Insights Aluminium industry depends heavily on cheap electricity. Kerala was an early centre due to hydropower availability. INDAL later merged with Hindalco. Aluminium is a strategic metal for industry.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the 2025 Formula One World Championship: i) Max Verstappen lost his championship by finishing second in the Abu Dhabi Grand Prix. ii) Lando Norris secured his first-ever World Drivers' Championship by winning the Abu Dhabi Grand Prix, marking his eighth victory of the 2025 season. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Lando Norris: Finished 3rd in Abu Dhabi to secure his first World Championship. Max Verstappen: Won the Abu Dhabi Grand Prix (1st place).lost by 2 points in world championship McLaren: Secured their first Drivers' Championship in over a decade. And also won constructor championship consecutive years 2024 and 2025

questions.question: Examine the statement below and select the appropirate options Assertion (A): The majority of India's iron ore is found in the form of high-grade lumps, making the process of pelletization unnecessary for the domestic steel industry. Reason (R): Pelletization is a process used to convert large, solid ore rocks into a fine dust-like powder to improve the efficiency of burning in a blast furnace.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) - False India's iron ore resources are not predominantly high-grade lumps. A large share exists as fines and lower-grade ore, which cannot be directly used efficiently in blast furnaces. Hence, pelletization is very much necessary for India's domestic steel industry to improve ore quality and usability. Reason (R) - False Pelletization does not convert large solid rocks into dust. Instead, it converts iron ore fines (powder) into uniform, spherical pellets. These pellets improve permeability, efficiency, and productivity in blast furnaces. Further Insights Pelletization improves blast furnace efficiency and output. India is among the top producers of iron ore, but quality varies widely. Iron ore lumps, fines, and pellets are the three main usable forms. Pellet usage reduces wastage and pollution. Steel plants increasingly rely on beneficiation and pellet plants.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the traditional practice and terminology of Judo: i) Ukemi is the foundational skill of falling safely, which includes techniques like Ushiro-ukemi (forward fall) and Mae-ukemi (back fall). ii) In a practice session, the person who initiates and performs the technique is known as Uke, while the person who receives the technique is known as Tori. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement (i) - Incorrect Ukemi indeed refers to the art of falling safely in Judo. However: Mae-ukemi = forward fall Ushiro-ukemi = backward fall The statement reverses these directions, making it incorrect. Statement (ii) - Incorrect In Judo practice: Tori = the person who executes/initiates the technique Uke = the person who receives the technique The statement interchanges the roles, so it is wrong. Further Insights Ukemi is essential for injury prevention in Judo. Judo emphasizes technique over brute strength. Terminology in martial arts is often exam-trap based. Uke - Tori roles are common across grappling martial arts.

questions.question: Which global aerospace company has set up the world's largest LEAP Engine MRO facility in Hyderabad?
questions.explanation: Safran has established the world's largest Maintenance, Repair and Overhaul (MRO) facility for LEAP engines in Hyderabad. The facility services LEAP-1A and LEAP-1B engines, widely used in next-generation narrow-body aircraft. This strengthens India's position as a global aviation MRO hub. Further Insights LEAP engines are known for fuel efficiency and lower emissions. The facility supports India's "Make in India" and aerospace ecosystem. Hyderabad is emerging as a major aerospace manufacturing cluster. MRO hubs reduce airline maintenance costs and turnaround time. Such investments boost high-skill employment in India.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of Indian rivers and their ancient names is NOT correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Vipasha is the ancient name of the Beas River, not the Ravi. The ancient name of the Ravi River is Parushni. Further Insights Most ancient river names come from Rigvedic texts. The Indus system rivers were crucial to early Vedic settlements. Shatadru literally means "river of a hundred streams." Parushni is associated with the Battle of Ten Kings.

questions.question: Which satellite mission is designed to provide high-resolution data for detecting tiny ground movements and issuing early warnings for natural disasters?
questions.explanation: NISAR (NASA - ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar) uses dual-frequency radar (L-band and S-band). It can detect minute ground deformations (millimetre-level), making it ideal for tracking earthquakes, landslides, volcanoes, floods, and subsidence. It provides all-weather, day-night observations, which is critical for disaster monitoring. RISAT-1A It is a radar imaging satellite mainly focused on agriculture, soil moisture, and crop monitoring. Cartosat-3 It is a high-resolution optical imaging satellite used for mapping, urban planning, and defence. Further Insights NISAR will map nearly the entire Earth every 12 days. Radar satellites can see through clouds and darkness, unlike optical satellites. NISAR will support climate change and cryosphere research. It strengthens India - USA space cooperation in Earth observation.

questions.question: Which architectural style is known as a 'hybrid' or 'mixed' style because it combines features of both Nagara and Dravida designs?
questions.explanation: Vesara architecture developed mainly in the Deccan region. It combines the curvilinear shikhara of the Nagara style with the pyramidal vimana and planning principles of the Dravida style. Hence, it is often described as a hybrid or mixed architectural style. Kalinga Kalinga style is a regional variation of Nagara architecture, mainly found in Odisha. Pagoda Pagoda architecture is associated with East and Southeast Asia, especially Buddhist structures. Gothic Gothic is a European architectural style known for pointed arches and flying buttresses. Further Insights Vesara style flourished under the Chalukyas and Hoysalas. It is prominent in Karnataka and adjoining Deccan regions. Nagara style dominates North India, Dravida in South India. Vesara temples often show innovative ground plans.

questions.question: The Malabar Exercise, which began as a bilateral naval drill between India and the USA in 1992, has evolved into a multilateral exercise involving all members of which strategic grouping?
questions.explanation: The Malabar Exercise initially involved India and the United States. Over time, Japan (permanent since 2015) and Australia (rejoined in 2020) became regular participants. These four countries together form the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (QUAD). ASEAN ASEAN is a regional economic and political grouping of Southeast Asian nations. BRICS BRICS focuses on economic cooperation among emerging economies (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa). SCO SCO is a Eurasian political and security organization led by China and Russia. Further Insights Malabar Exercise emphasizes maritime security and freedom of navigation. It strengthens interoperability among QUAD navies. The exercise reflects rising focus on the Indo-Pacific region. Japan became a permanent participant in 2015. Australia's inclusion in 2020 marked Malabar's full QUAD character.

questions.question: Which government initiative, launched in 2025, aims to create about 3.5 crore jobs by providing EPFO-linked incentives to employers and first-time employees?
questions.explanation: · On July 1, 2025 , the Union Cabinet approved an employment-linked incentive (ELI) scheme , named the Pradhan Mantri Vikasit Bharat Rojgar Yojana (PMVBRY). · Its objective is to promote employment generation , enhance employability, and strengthen social security across all sectors, including micro , small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) and rural enterprises , with a special focus on the manufacturing sector. · Incentives under the Pradhan Mantri Vikasit Bharat Rojgar Yojana (PMVBRY) will apply to jobs created during the two-year registration period, starting August 1, 2025 , and ending July 31 , 2027. · The scheme aims to stimulate the creation of over 35 million jobs across the country over a two-year period. The scheme has a budgetary outlay of ₹ 99,446 crore. · Under Part A of the scheme, first - time employees in both existing and new establishments will receive an incentive equivalent to one month's Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) salary , subject to a maximum limit of ₹ 15,000 . · This incentive will be paid in two installments during the first year of employment. The first installment , amounting to ₹ 7,500 , will be paid after six months of continuous employment. · Eligibility for the second installment will be granted after completing twelve months of employment and successfully completing the prescribed financial literacy program. · This second installment will be deposited into an appropriate savings instrument/savings account for a specified period. Under Part B , employers will receive an incentive of up to ₹ 3,000 per month for each additional employment created for at least six months. PM Vishwakarma It focuses on artisans and traditional craftspeople. It provides skill training, toolkit support, and credit PM JAY PM-JAY (Ayushman Bharat) is a health insurance scheme. PM SVANidhi PM SVANidhi supports street vendors through working capital loans. Further Insights EPFO linkage promotes formalisation of the workforce. Employment-linked incentives reduce the cost of hiring for employers. First-time employees gain social security coverage early. The scheme aligns with India's demographic dividend strategy. Formal job creation improves long-term tax and pension coverage.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the methods of heat transfer: i) Convection transfers heat without the actual movement of molecules. ii) Conduction involves heat transfer by the bulk movement of fluids. iii) Radiation can transfer heat through a vacuum without any medium. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement (iii) is correct: Radiation does not require any material medium and can occur through a vacuum (e.g., heat from the Sun reaching Earth). Statement (i) - Incorrect Convection always involves actual movement of molecules in fluids (liquids and gases). Heat is carried by the motion of matter itself. Statement (ii) - Incorrect Conduction occurs without bulk movement of matter and mainly in solids. Bulk movement of fluids is a feature of convection, not conduction. Further Insights Conduction dominates in solids. Convection occurs only in liquids and gases. Radiation involves electromagnetic waves. Sun's heat reaches Earth by radiation. Heat transfer concepts are very high-frequency prelims topics.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Kushana Dynasty: i) KujulaKadphises was the first major ruler who united the five tribes of the Yuezhi people. ii) Vima Kadphises expanded the empire into the Indian interior, reaching as far as Mathura. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement (i) is correct: KujulaKadphises successfully unified the five Yuezhi tribes, laying the foundation of the Kushana political power. Statement (ii) is correct: Vima Kadphises expanded Kushana control deep into north-western India, and Mathura emerged as a major political and economic centre under Kushana rule. Further Insights The Kushanas controlled key sections of the Silk Route. Mathura became a centre of trade, administration, and art. Vima Kadphises issued gold coins, indicating prosperity. Kushanas acted as a bridge between Central Asian and Indian cultures. The dynasty reached its zenith under Kanishka.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution is the Governor empowered to promulgate ordinances when the State Legislature is not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 213 empowers the Governor to promulgate Ordinances when the State Legislature (or either House, in case of a bicameral legislature) is not in session. Such ordinances have the same force as an Act of the State Legislature, but are temporary in nature. Article 123 Article 123 gives ordinance-making power to the President Article 153 Article 153 deals with the office of the Governor for each State. Article 161 Article 161 relates to the pardoning powers of the Governor. Further Insights Governor's ordinance power is co-extensive with the State Legislature's law-making power. Ordinances must be approved within six weeks of reassembly of the Legislature. Ordinance-making is a temporary emergency power. Excessive use of ordinances has been criticised by courts.

questions.question: What does a "Convertible" debenture allow an investor to turn their debt into after a specified period?
questions.explanation: A convertible debenture is a type of debt instrument that can be converted into equity shares of the issuing company after a specified period or on specified terms. This feature allows investors to benefit from both fixed interest (initially) and ownership in the company (later). Further Insights Non-convertible debentures (NCDs) cannot be converted into shares. Convertible debentures usually offer lower interest rates. Conversion reduces the company's debt burden. Investors gain from potential share price appreciation.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the "Drain of Wealth" theory: i) It was popularized by Dadabhai Naoroji to explain the systematic transfer of India's resources to Britain. ii) The "Home Charges" mentioned in this theory included payments for salaries and pensions of British officials. iii) This intellectual critique served as a foundational element for the Early Nationalist Phase of the Indian National Congress. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement I is correct Because Dadabhai Naoroji systematically explained how colonial rule led to a continuous outflow of wealth from India to Britain without adequate return. Statement II is correct Home Charges included expenses such as salaries, pensions of British civil and military officials, interest on public debt, and administrative costs paid from Indian revenues. Statement III is correct since the Drain of Wealth theory became the economic backbone of Early Nationalist criticism, shaping the demands and resolutions of the early Indian National Congress. Further Insights The theory highlighted colonial economic exploitation, not administrative inefficiency. It strengthened demands for Indianisation of services. Naoroji called Britain's rule an "un-British" system. The idea influenced later leaders like R.C. Dutt. Economic critique preceded the demand for political self-rule.

questions.question: Which title did Babur assume after defeating the Rajput confederacy led by Rana Sanga in the Battle of Khanwa?
questions.explanation: After his victory over Rana Sanga at the Battle of Khanwa (1527), Babur declared the conflict as a religious war (jihad). He assumed the title "Ghazi", meaning a warrior or champion of Islam, to motivate his troops and consolidate support. Further Insights The Battle of Khanwa weakened Rajput power in North India. Babur used gunpowder and field artillery effectively at Khanwa. Khanwa followed Babur's victory at the First Battle of Panipat (1526). The title Ghazi was used to inspire Islamic warriors. Khanwa secured the foundation of Mughal rule in India.

questions.question: Which goddess is the Bonalu festival of Telangana dedicated to?
questions.explanation: Bonalu is a traditional festival of Telangana dedicated to Goddess Mahakali, worshipped as a protector deity. The festival involves offering Bonam (a ritual pot with cooked rice, jaggery, curd, etc.) to the goddess, seeking protection from diseases and calamities. Further Insights Bonalu is celebrated mainly in Hyderabad and Secunderabad. It is observed during the Ashada month (July - August). The festival reflects folk traditions and Shakta worship. Women play a central role in Bonalu rituals. Bonalu was declared the State Festival of Telangana.

questions.question: Which traditional martial dance features performers using a sword and a shield in a series of rhythmic mock fights?
questions.explanation: Thang-Ta is a traditional martial dance and combat art of Manipur. Thang means sword and Ta means shield. The performance involves rhythmic mock fights, combining martial techniques with dance movements. Gatka Gatka is a martial art of Punjab, mainly associated with Sikh warriors. Kalaripayattu Kalaripayattu originates in Kerala. It includes body movements, weapons, and healing techniques, Silambam Silambam is a Tamil martial art primarily using bamboo staffs. Further Insights Thang-Ta evolved from ancient warfare traditions of Manipur. It is closely linked with Manipuri festivals and rituals. Martial dances preserve indigenous combat heritage. Many Indian martial arts combine dance and warfare techniques.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the new provisions introduced under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 (BNSS), 2023: i) It introduces "Community Service" as a specific form of punishment for minor offences. ii) It mandates that forensic evidence collection at a crime scene must be video-recorded, preferably using a mobile phone. iii) It allows for "Trial in Absentia", enabling courts to proceed with trials even if the accused is a proclaimed offender who has absconded. iv) It establishes that first-time undertrial offenders can be released on bail after serving two-thirds of their maximum possible sentence. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement (i) is correct: BNSS introduces Community Service as a punishment for certain minor offences, marking a shift towards reformative justice. Statement (ii) is correct: The law mandates video recording of forensic evidence collection, ensuring transparency, accountability, and reduced tampering. Statement (iii) is correct: BNSS allows Trial in Absentia, enabling courts to deliver judgments even when proclaimed offenders evade the process of law. Statement (iv) is incorrect Under BNSS, first-time undertrial offenders may be released on bail after serving one-third (not two-thirds) of the maximum possible sentence for the offence. Further Insights BNSS replaces the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC), 1973. Videography strengthens evidence admissibility in courts. Trial in absentia discourages wilful absconding by accused persons. Community service reflects a rehabilitative approach to justice. Early bail provisions reduce overcrowding of prisons.

questions.question: A sum of ₹20,000 is invested at compound interest at 15% for the first year, 10% for the second year, and 12% for the third year. What is the amount at the end of 3 years?
questions.explanation: Amount after first year = 20000 (1 + 15/100) = ₹23000 Amount after second year = 23000 (1 + 10/100) = ₹25300 Amount after third year = 25300 (1 + 12/100) = ₹28336

questions.question: A student got 112 marks in English, 102 marks in Hindi, 98 marks in Biology, and 88 marks in Maths. If the maximum marks of each subject are 120, then find the overall percentage of marks get.
questions.explanation: Total marks = 112 + 102 + 98 + 88 = 400 Hence, the required percentage = 83.33%

questions.question: Zahir has 56 litres of a dye solution at 56% strength. He removes x litres of the well-stirred mixture and replaces it with water once, obtaining a 42% solution. Find x.
questions.explanation: Initial dye content = 56% of 56 = 31.36 litres Let, x litres of the mixture be removed. Dye removed = 56% of x = 0.56x litres Remaining dye after removal = (31.36 - 0.56x) litres Final dye content = 42% of 56 = 0.42 56 = 23.52 litres So, 31.36 - 0.56x = 23.52 => 0.56x = 7.84 => x = 14

questions.question: If the line y = 11x + 24 passes through (X, 2), then find the value of X.
questions.explanation: Since the line y = 11x + 24 passes through (X, 2), then, by putting, x = X and y = 2 => 2 = 11X + 24 => -22 = 11X => X = -2

questions.question: If H liters of water mixed with G liters of wine costing Rs. 240 per liters such that a profit of 25% by selling the mixture at Rs. 275 per liters, then what is the ratio of H to G?
questions.explanation: Cost price of the mixture = 275 * 100/125 = Rs. 220 per liter Total cost price = (H + G) * 220 => H * 0 + G * 240 = 220H + 220G => 20G = 220H => H : G = 20 : 220 => H : G = 1: 11

questions.question: A, B and C invest Rs.80000, Rs.120000 and Rs.160000. The firm makes a loss of Rs.360000. What is A's share of loss?

Three workers - A, B and C have efficiencies in the ratio 1 : 2 : 3. A works for 6 days, then B works alone for 4 days. Finally B and C together finish the remaining work in 5 days. How many days would A alone take to complete the entire work?
questions.explanation: Let, A's work-rate = 1 unit/day, B's work-rate = 2 units/day, C's work-rate = 3 units/day Work done by A in 6 days = 6 X 1 = 6 units Work done by B in 4 days = 4 X 2 = 8 units Work done by B and C in 5 days = (2 + 3) X 5 = 25 units Total work = 6 + 8 + 25 = 39 units Time for A alone = 39 ÷ 1 = 39 days

questions.question: If y + 1/y = 7, find the value of y3 + 1/y3.
Evaluate: (√625 + 142) ÷ (√81 + √64).
questions.explanation: Compute numerator: √625 = 25, 142 = 196 → 25 + 196 = 221. Compute denominator: √81 = 9, √64 = 8 → 9 + 8 = 17. Value = 221 ÷ 17 = 13.

questions.question: After giving two successive discounts of 12% each on the marked price, and item was sold at Rs.968. What is the marked price of the item?
questions.explanation: Equivalent discount for two successive discounts of 12% each = (12 + 12 - 12 * 12/100)% = 22.56% Selling price of item = Rs.968 Therefore, cost price of item = 968 * 100/(100 - 22.56) = Rs.1250

questions.question: In the given figure, what is the supplementary angle of (∠X + ∠Y)? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14245/image007.png)
questions.explanation: Consider major arc AB, Angle subtended by arc AB on circle = 60° Therefore, angle subtended by arc on centre = 2* 60° = 120° Now consider arc minor AB, ∠AOB = 360° - 120° = 240° In quadrilateral AOBC, Sum of all angles = 360° X + Y + 240° + 60° = 360° X + Y = 360° - 300° X + Y = 60° Supplementary angle of (∠X + ∠Y) = 180° - 60° = 120°.

questions.question: A and B invest ₹60,000 and ₹1,20,000 respectively. A invested for 12 months. If they share profits equally, for how many months did B invest?
questions.explanation: A time - capital = 60000 X 12 = 720000 Let B's months = x => time - capital = 120000 X x Equal share => 720000 = 120000 X x => x = 6

questions.question: A man bought 10 kg of apples at a certain amount. He sold 5 kg apples at 10% loss, 3 kg apples at 5% profit and 2 kg apples at p% profit. If his overall loss percentage is 1.5%, then what is the value of 'p'?
questions.explanation: Let CP of 1 kg apple = ₹100 CP of 10 kg apples = 100*10 = ₹1000 Total SP = SP of 5 kg apples + SP of 3 kg apples + SP of 2 kg apples = 90% of 5*100 + 105% of 3*100 + (100+p)% of 2*100 = 450 + 315 + 200 + 2p = 965 + 2p Loss = 1.5% [(1000 - 965 - 2p)/1000]*100 = 1.5 2p = 35 - 15 p = 10

questions.question: Simplify: (49D2 - 16) ÷ (7D - 4)2.
Profit percentage earned after selling an article for Rs. 2425 is equal to the loss percentage incurred after selling the article for Rs. 1455. Find cost price of the article.
questions.explanation: Let the cost price of the article = Rs. X According to question, (2425 - X)/X * 100 = (X - 1455)/X * 100 2425 - X = X - 1455 2X = 3880 X = Rs. 1940

questions.question: A can finish a work in 15 days and B in 20 days. They worked together for x days; then B alone finished the remaining work in 6 days. Find x.
questions.explanation: Let, total work = LCM of 15 and 20 = 60 units A's one day work = 60 / 15 = 4 units B's one day work = 60 / 20 = 3 units Work done by A and B together in one day = 4 + 3 = 7 units Work done together in x days = 7x units Work done by B in 6 days = 3 6 = 18 units So, 7x + 18 = 60 => 7x = 42 => x = 6

questions.question: A car goes from R to S at 72 km/h and returns from S to R at 90 km/h. If the total time for the round trip is 9 hours, what is the one-way distance of RS (in km)?
questions.explanation: Let, the one-way distance RS = d km Time taken from R to S = (d/72) hours Time taken from S to R = (d/90) hours Total time for the round trip = 9 hours So, d/72 + d/90 = 9 => (5d + 4d) / 360 = 9 => 9d / 360 = 9 => d = 360 km

questions.question: The area of an equilateral triangle is 49√3 sq. cm. Find the circumference of a circle whose radius equals the side of this triangle.
questions.explanation: Let, the side of the equilateral triangle be a cm. (√3 / 4) X a² = 49√3 (1 / 4) X a² = 49 a² = 49 X 4 = 196 a = √196 = 14 cm Circumference of the circle = 2 X (22/7) X 14 = 88 cm

questions.question: What is the effective annual rate corresponding to a nominal rate of 10% p.a. compounded half-yearly?
Evaluate: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er260122st11.png)
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28572/image040.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28572/image041.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28572/image042.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28572/image043.png) = 2/7

questions.question: After 8 examinations, a student's average is 62 marks. What must be the score in the 9th examination to raise the average to 64?
questions.explanation: Required score = 64 X 9 − 62 X 8 = 576 − 496 = 80

questions.question: An ant walks around a circular pond of radius 7 m for 15 complete rounds. How far does the ant travel? (Use π = 3.14.)
questions.explanation: Let r = 7 m Circumference = 2πr = 2 X 3.14 X 7 = 43.96 m Distance = 15 X 43.96 = 659.40 m

questions.question: A firm's 50 employees' average salary is ₹30,000. Ten managers averaging ₹75,000 join. What is the new overall average?
questions.explanation: Sum for employees' salary: 50 X 30,000 = 1,500,000 Sum for managers' salary: 10 X 75,000 = 750,000 Total Sum: 1,500,000 + 750,000 = 2,250,000 Total Count: 50 + 10 = 60 New Average: 2,250,000 ÷ 60 = ₹37,500

questions.question: Find the ratio in lowest terms of 9216 and 12800.
questions.explanation: Ratio in lowest terms of 9216 and 12800 = 9216 : 12800 = 18 : 25

questions.question: A car is 450 m behind a bike. In one minute, the car travels 100 m while the bike travels 80 m. How long will it take the car to overtake the bike?
questions.explanation: Relative distance = 450 m Relative speed = (100 − 80) m/min = 20 m/min Required time = 450/20 = 22.5 min = 22 min 30 sec

questions.question: Identify the tense and voice used in the following sentence taken from the passage: "Easy words are already embedded in everyday language, which reduces the cognitive effort required for consumers to understand and remember the brand."
questions.explanation: In the sentence, "Easy words are already embedded in everyday language..", the verb structure "are embedded" is in the present simple tense and is passive in voice, as the subject "Easy words" receives the action rather than performing it. The verb "reduces" later in the sentence is active, but the tense and voice asked about clearly refer to "are embedded." Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What grammatical function does the phrase "that is intuitive and easy to pronounce" serve in the following sentence? "In the digital age, where consumers are constantly exposed to advertisements, apps, and notifications, a brand name that is intuitive and easy to pronounce has a higher chance of being remembered and searched for."
questions.explanation: The phrase "that is intuitive and easy to pronounce" modifies the noun "brand name." It begins with the relative pronoun "that" and provides additional information about the noun it follows. Therefore, it functions as an adjective clause, not an adverbial or noun clause. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. Note: An adjective clause (also called a relative clause) is a dependent clause that acts as an adjective in a sentence. It modifies (describes or gives more information about) a noun or pronoun, usually starting with a relative pronoun like who, whom, whose, which, that, or a relative adverb like where, when, or why. Examples: The book that I borrowed is on the table. (modifies "book") The student who won the award is my friend. (modifies "student") This is the house where I grew up. (modifies "house")

questions.question: Choose the word closest in meaning to the word "approachable" as used in the passage.
questions.explanation: In the passage, "approachable" describes a brand that feels friendly, easy to engage with, and consumer centric. Among the given options, "accessible" is closest in meaning, as it suggests ease of use and openness. The other options convey meanings opposite to the context. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: According to the passage, what is the primary advantage of using simple, familiar words in brand naming?
options: It makes the brand appear more luxurious and exclusive.
It reduces cognitive effort and improves clarity and understanding.
It helps the brand avoid legal trademark issues completely.
It ensures the brand remains unknown to competitors.
questions.explanation: As stated in the passage: "This method works because it prioritises clarity over complexity. Easy words are already embedded in everyday language, which reduces the cognitive effort required for consumers to understand and remember the brand." (paragraph 1) The passage further explains that combinations like MakeMyTrip and BookMyShow "instantly convey what the service does," emphasizing that the main benefit is improved clarity and easier understanding. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the overall tone of the passage?
questions.explanation: The passage systematically explains a branding strategy by outlining its advantages - clarity, memorability, scalability, and emotional connection - using logical reasoning and examples. It neither criticises nor narrates personal anecdotes. Hence, the overall tone is best described as analytical and informative. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Make hay while the sun shines
options: to work hard until a certain age
to earn a lot of money in a small amount of time
to take advantage of a good situation until one can
questions.explanation: The idiom 'make hay while the sun shines' means to take advantage of a good situation until it is possible. Other options are incorrect. Option c) is the right answer.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling and meaning of the correct answer is: Harass: to continue to annoy or upset someone over a period of time. Thus, the correct answer is option (b). Judgement - the ability to make considered decisions or come to sensible conclusions. Intelligence - the ability to acquire and apply knowledge and skills. Hygiene - conditions or practices conducive to maintaining health and preventing disease, especially through cleanliness.

questions.question: Find the synonym of "Obscure".
questions.explanation: The word "obscure" means unclear or not easily understood. (a) "Clear" is the opposite of "obscure". (b) "Vague" means unclear, which aligns with "obscure". (c) "Bright" relates to light. (d) "Proud" relates to emotion. Hence, "vague" is the correct synonym.

questions.question: Select the correctly spelt word to fill in the blank. The teacher noticed a _____ expression on Tim's face.
questions.explanation: Quizzical - This is the correct spelling. "Quizzical" means expressing curiosity, puzzlement, or amusement, and it fits appropriately in the sentence. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. The final sentence: The teacher noticed a quizzical expression on Tim's face.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in reported speech. The professor asked, "Can you do your best, Latika?"
options: The professor wanted to know if Latika could do her best.
The professor asked if Latika can do her best.
The professor asked could Latika do her best.
The professor asked if Latika could do her best.
questions.explanation: The reported verb ('asked') is in past tense, appropriate changes would be made to the reported speech. The modals are changed in their past tense form in indirect speech. 'Can' will change to 'could'. Therefore, option (b) is eliminated. The indirect speech is introduced by verbs as 'asked', 'inquired' etc. Hence, option (a) is eliminated. When the question doesn't start with a 'wh-' word, the reporting verb is followed by an 'if' or 'whether'. Hence, the correct sentence in indirect speech is: The professor asked if Latika could do her best. Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Find the synonym of "Quiet".
questions.explanation: The word "quiet" means making little or no noise. (a) "Noisy" is the opposite of "quiet". (b) "Calm" reflects peacefulness, matching "quiet". (c) "Rapid" relates to speed. (d) "Clever" relates to intelligence. Thus, "calm" is the correct synonym.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of "Bitter".
questions.explanation: The word "bitter" refers to a sharp, unpleasant taste or, figuratively, a feeling of resentment. (a) "Sour" describes another unpleasant taste and is similar in sense to "bitter". (b) "Gentle" means mild or kind in nature and is unrelated to taste or emotion. (c) "Sweet" refers to a pleasant taste or agreeable nature and stands in direct contrast to "bitter". (d) "Elastic" means flexible and stretchable and has no connection with taste or emotion. Therefore, "sweet" is the correct antonym of "bitter", making option (c) the correct choice.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Laugh in one's sleeves
options: to laugh at someone else's misery
questions.explanation: Laugh in one's sleeves: to laugh secretly. The rest of the meanings are contextually incorrect. Therefore, the correct answer is: (b) to laugh secretly.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The Big Bang, in addition to the other interesting topics, were included in the syllabus last year.
questions.explanation: The error lies in option (c). If two subjects are separated by 'in addition to', the verb agrees with the main subject. For example: Ronaldo, in addition to Messi, is included in the Ballon d'Or list. (Here, 'Ronaldo' is the main subject. Therefore, the verb 'is' agrees with it.) Similarly, in this context, 'the Big Bang' is the main subject. As the subject is singular, the verb that must agree with it must also be singular. Hence, 'were' [plural] must be replaced by 'was' [singular]. Thus, the error is in option (c) were included in. Therefore, the corrected form of the sentence is: The Big Bang, in addition to the other interesting topics, was included in the syllabus last year. Additional Information: If two subjects are separated by anything except "and", the verb agrees with the main subject. The list of such words is:As well as Like Unlike Besides In addition to With Together with Along with Including Accompanied by But

questions.question: Spot the erroneous parts, if any, in the following sentence: Even after winning (A)/ the toss, / the captain decided (B)/ to field (C)/ No error (D).
questions.explanation: The parts of the sentence are grammatically and contextually correct. Hence, the correct answer is option (c).

questions.question: Choose the most suitable option to replace the highlighted part of the sentence: What she liked most was the book he recommending.
options: was the book that he recommended
is the book that he recommends
had been the book that he had recommended
would be the book that he had recommended
questions.explanation: The grammar concept of "cleft sentences and tense concord" applies here. In cleft constructions like "What she liked most was...", the relative clause must have a tense consistent with the main clause. The highlighted part is wrong because "he recommending" is in the present continuous tense, though ungrammatical; the relative clause does not match the past tense of "liked." Option (a) - correct "was the book that he recommended" matches the past tense "liked"; tense concord between the main clause and relative clause is maintained. Option (b) - incorrect Present tense "recommends" conflicts with past tense "liked," creating a temporal mismatch. Option (c) - incorrect Double past perfect ("had been... had recommended") is unnecessary; it changes the temporal reference. Option (d) - incorrect Conditional "would be" is misaligned with past simple "liked." Hence, option (a) is correct. Note: In cleft sentences, tense concord ensures that relative clauses reflect the same temporal reference as the main clause, preventing temporal inconsistencies.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences is grammatically correct?
options: The price of the books are high.
The price of the book is high.
The prices of the book are high.
The prices of the books is high.
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is (b). The prepositional phrase "of the book" modifies "price" (singular subject), so the verb is singular "is." Option (a) is incorrect because the subject "price" is singular, not suitable with the plural verb "are." Option (c) is incorrect because "prices" (plural) doesn't match the singular "book." Option (d) is incorrect as the plural subject "prices" would need the plural verb "are". Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. End this fight now!
options: Now must this fight be ended.
This fight must be ended now.
You must end this fight now.
Must this fight be ended now.
questions.explanation: Imperative Sentences having ‘object’ are converted into passive voice using following structure: Let + Object + be + V3. If let structure is not given, use this structure — Object + must + be + V3. The subject of active voice becomes the object in passive voice and vice-versa. So, the correct sentence in passive voice becomes: 'This fight must be ended now'. Hence, option b) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The homophone for the word 'coarse' is _________.
questions.explanation: Homophones are words that sound the same but have different meanings and spellings. "Coarse" and "course" are pronounced the same, but they have different meanings: Coarse - rough or harsh in texture. Course - a path, direction, or progression (e.g., "a golf course" or "the course of events"); an academic subject of study (e.g., "a math course"). Hence, option (b) is the answer. Calls - To shout or summon; phone conversations or cries. Courts - Areas for sports like tennis or basketball; legal venues or systems for justice. Coats - Outer garments worn for warmth; layers of material covering a surface.

questions.question: Choose the correct semantic fit. The team's innovative design earned them _____ in the industry awards.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (b) recognition. "Recognition" refers to acknowledgment or praise for achievement, such as winning industry awards. In this context, it semantically matches the positive outcome of the team's innovative work. (a) ridicule - is incorrect because "ridicule" means mockery or derision, suggesting negative judgment, not celebratory success. (c) obscurity - is incorrect because "obscurity" implies lack of fame or notice, which contradicts earning awards. (d) hesitation - is incorrect because "hesitation" refers to uncertainty or delay, unrelated to the accomplishment of awards. Complete sentence: The team's innovative design earned them recognition in the industry awards.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a phrase is given. From the options, choose the one that sums up the meaning of the given phrase in one word. A person who dislikes humankind and avoids human society
questions.explanation: Misandrist - a person who hates men. Misogynist - a person who hates women. Misanthrope - a person who hates humans. Philanthropist - a person who seeks to promote the welfare of others, especially by the generous donation of money for good causes. Option (c) is the right answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Rapacious
questions.explanation: "Rapacious" means aggressively greedy or wanting more than one needs. It suggests a strong desire to acquire or possess something, often in an exploitative way. "Content" means satisfied or at ease, which is the opposite of "rapacious". Hence, option (a) is the answer. Poor: This is not an antonym because being poor does not directly oppose being rapacious, which is about greed rather than material wealth. Privileged: This refers to having advantages or special rights, but it doesn't directly counter the meaning of "rapacious". Influential: This refers to having power or influence, which is not an opposite of "rapacious".

questions.question: Rearrange the following stages in the formulation of a national budget in the correct order: P. Parliamentary discussion and approval of budget proposals. Q. Assessment of economic conditions and revenue projections. R. Implementation of budgetary provisions during the financial year. S. Preparation of expenditure estimates by ministries.
questions.explanation: Budget formulation begins with assessing the overall economic situation and expected revenues (Q). Ministries then prepare detailed expenditure estimates (S). These proposals are debated and approved by the legislature (P). Finally, the approved budget is implemented during the financial year (R). Thus, the correct order is Q → S → P → R. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following question, the first and the last parts of the sentence/passage are numbered 1 and 6. The rest of the sentence/passage is split into four parts - P, Q, R, and S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find the most logical order of the parts between 1 and 6. 1: The invention of the printing press transformed the spread of knowledge in Europe. P. This led to increased literacy and wider access to education. Q. Printed books could be produced faster and at a lower cost than handwritten manuscripts. R. As a result, ideas began to circulate beyond elite circles. S. Prior to this, information was largely controlled by religious and scholarly institutions. 6: The printing press thus played a crucial role in shaping modern society.
questions.explanation: The passage traces the impact of the printing press. After the introduction in (1), (S) explains the earlier situation. (Q) follows by describing the technological change. (R) logically shows the consequence of mass printing. (P) then explains the social outcome of this spread. Sentence (6) concludes the overall impact. Thus, the correct sequence is SQRP. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. The manager told the team members that he was expecting better results.
options: The manager said to the team members, "I will expect better results."
The manager said to the team members, "I was expecting better results."
The manager said to the team members, "I am expecting better results."
The manager says to the team members, "I am expecting better results."
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Indirect Speech is in Past Continuous Tense. Therefore, the sentence in the Direct Speech will be in Present Continuous Tense. Past Continuous (Subject + was/were + V1 + ing + Object) will be converted into Present Continuous (Subject + is/am/are + V1 +ing + Object). For example: He said that Suman was teaching the kids. (Indirect Speech) He said, "Suman is teaching the kids." (Direct Speech) Also, option (d) is incorrect because the tense of reporting verb doesn't change, so the conversion of 'told' into 'says to' is incorrect. The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect Speech to Direct Speech:Indirect SpeechDirect Speech told... thatsaid to He wasI am Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.
